top_stories.label: Top Stories
top_stories.ai_summary: Markets are jittery after President Trump returned from Beijing with handshakes but no real deals — and oil jumped above $109 a barrel as ships were attacked and seized in the Strait of Hormuz. Wall Street fell across the board on inflation and bond-yield fears. Outside Asia, Cuba is in the dark literally: it has run out of diesel and Havana is enduring 22-hour blackouts. The dominant theme today is a tense, expensive standoff — between superpowers, in the oil markets, and in countries left without fuel.

stories.headline: Trump leaves Beijing without breakthroughs; Taiwan arms package still on the table
stories.summary: President Trump wrapped up a two-day visit to Beijing with Xi Jinping, returning home with a tentative Boeing order but no major agreements on Iran, trade, or Taiwan. Trump told reporters he has not yet decided whether to approve the pending US arms sale to Taiwan, calling it a 'very good negotiating chip'. He also said he raised a three-way US–Russia–China nuclear talks idea with Xi.

stories.headline: Strait of Hormuz tensions spike: ship seized off UAE, another sunk near Oman
stories.summary: A commercial vessel anchored off the United Arab Emirates was seized on May 15 and taken toward Iran, while a second ship was attacked and sank near the coast of Oman. The incidents are the sharpest escalation since the fragile US–Iran ceasefire signed in April and pushed Brent crude up more than 3% to $109 a barrel.

stories.headline: Cuba completely out of diesel and fuel oil; Havana blackouts hit 22 hours a day
stories.summary: Cuba's energy minister Vicente de la O Levy said on May 14 the country has 'absolutely no fuel, oil, and absolutely no diesel'. Many Havana neighbourhoods are without power for 20–22 hours a day, and protests have broken out in the western areas around the capital. The UN warns the resulting water and hospital disruptions threaten food security.

stories.headline: Wall Street tumbles as oil surges and Treasury yields hit 1-year high
stories.summary: The S&P 500 fell 1.24% to 7,408.50, the Dow lost 537 points (-1.07%), and the Nasdaq dropped 1.54%. The 10-year Treasury yield jumped to 4.55% — the highest in a year — as rising oil prices from the Hormuz crisis reignited inflation fears. Boeing slid 2.8% after a China order came in smaller than markets had hoped.

stories.headline: Putin hints Ukraine war 'coming to an end' — Kyiv unconvinced as 200 drones strike overnight
stories.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin suggested his country's war on Ukraine may be 'coming to an end' and offered to meet Zelenskyy in a third country. Hours after a US-brokered three-day truce expired, Russia launched more than 200 drones at Ukraine. Zelenskyy responded that 'Russia has no intention of ending this war' and that Kyiv is bracing for more attacks.

stories.headline: Drones now cause 80% of civilian deaths in Sudan's fourth year of war
stories.summary: The UN says drones have caused more than 80% of civilian deaths in Sudan in the first four months of 2026, killing at least 880 people. A May 8 strike on Al Quz in South Kordofan reportedly killed 26 civilians. The SAF and RSF have begun pre-negotiation talks in Jeddah, but fighting in Darfur and Kordofan continues to escalate.

stories.headline: UK bans seven foreign far-right activists ahead of London rally
stories.summary: Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood barred seven foreign far-right figures, including US influencer Valentina Gomez, from entering the UK ahead of a London rally led by Tommy Robinson on May 16. Prime Minister Keir Starmer said 'agitators' will not be allowed in to inflame tensions.

singapore.label: Singapore
singapore.ai_summary: Wet afternoons are the main story locally — NEA is forecasting short, sharp thundery showers most afternoons through the rest of May, and PUB issued a flash-flood advisory in Jurong West on Saturday morning. Dengue peak season is here, and MOH is reminding households to do the Mozzie Wipeout B-L-O-C-K routine. On the policy side, expanded CPF Housing Grants of up to S$40,000 for first-time buyers and new public-transport fare caps are the household-budget items to know about.

stories.headline: PUB flash-flood advisory: motorists told to avoid parts of Jurong West after morning deluge
stories.summary: PUB urged motorists to avoid affected stretches in Jurong West on the morning of May 16 after a sudden heavy downpour overwhelmed drainage. The agency continues to monitor 210 sensors across canals and drains as Sumatra squall season runs through the rest of May.

stories.headline: NEA: thundery showers expected on most afternoons through end-May
stories.summary: NEA's fortnightly outlook says inter-monsoon conditions will dominate the second half of May, with short-duration thundery showers on most afternoons and Sumatra squalls bringing gusty pre-dawn rain on some days. Most of Singapore has already recorded above-average rainfall this month, with 114.6mm in one afternoon near Tampines on May 8.

stories.headline: Dengue peak season — MOH urges households to keep up Mozzie Wipeout
stories.summary: With Singapore entering its annual dengue peak (May–October), MOH and NEA are pushing the B-L-O-C-K routine — Break up hardened soil, Lift and empty flowerpot plates, Overturn pails, Change vase water, Keep roof gutters clear. The updated National Dengue Clinical Guideline (Mar 2026) is now in use across hospitals.

stories.headline: Housing: enhanced CPF grant of up to S$40,000 for first-time buyers
stories.summary: Singapore is widening housing support for first-time buyers this year, headlined by the Enhanced CPF Housing Grant of up to S$40,000 for income-based support. It is stacked with the Single Singapore Citizen Grant (S$25,000), the Proximity Housing Grant (S$15,000) and an Additional Housing Grant of up to S$10,000 for special cases. National Development Minister Chee Hong Tat also confirmed an EC policy review on affordability.

stories.headline: Lower public-transport fare caps and wider subsidy tiers kick in
stories.summary: New transport measures in 2026 introduce a lower daily fare cap so commuters never spend above a fixed amount per day, and expand subsidies into middle-income households that previously did not qualify. The Land Transport and Related Matters Bill — covering active mobility, ERP 2.0 and vehicle safety — is also moving through Parliament.

stories.headline: 22-year-old man found dead on Siloso Beach, Sentosa
stories.summary: A 22-year-old man was found dead at Siloso Beach on Sentosa on the morning of May 15. Police said preliminary investigations indicate no foul play is suspected. Investigations are ongoing.

wars.label: Wars & Conflicts
wars.ai_summary: Three theatres are simmering at different temperatures. Russia–Ukraine is back to drone war after a brief US-brokered truce — Putin is talking peace, Kyiv isn't buying it. The US–Iran ceasefire is the most fragile: ships are being seized and sunk in the Strait of Hormuz, pushing oil above $109. Israel–Lebanon is officially in a ceasefire that just got extended 45 days, but Israeli strikes are still hitting southern Lebanon daily, with more than 2,800 killed since the war began.

theatres.name: Russia – Ukraine War
theatres.status: Active — drone war resumed after 3-day truce expired

timeline.event: Russia launches full-scale invasion of Ukraine.
War grinds into stalemate; Western military aid continues amid political tensions in Washington.
Russia launches over 8,000 drones in a single month — the highest monthly total since the invasion began.
Russia and Ukraine declare competing ceasefires around Victory Day.
US President Trump announces a 72-hour truce overlapping with Russia's Victory Day; both sides honour it briefly.
Putin says the war 'may be coming to an end' and offers to meet Zelenskyy in a third country.
Truce expires; Russia fires 200+ drones overnight at Ukraine. Ukraine retaliates by striking gas facilities in Russia's Orenburg region, 1,500km from the border.
Russia loses net 12 sq mi of territory in the past week — net 45 sq mi since mid-April.
Zelenskyy says Russia 'has no intention of ending this war' and warns of more attacks.

theatres.name: US/Israel – Iran War & Strait of Hormuz Crisis

timeline.event: US and Israel launch joint air war against Iran. Opening strike kills Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei; Iran retaliates with hundreds of missiles and thousands of drones across the region.
Iran declares the Strait of Hormuz 'closed' and threatens to attack any ship attempting to transit.
US and Iran agree on a ceasefire that includes Israel after roughly five weeks of fighting.
Ceasefire formally takes effect. Conflict shifts to brinkmanship over Strait access and Tehran's counter-demands (sovereignty over the Strait, war damages, asset releases, sanctions relief).
US and Iranian forces trade shots in the Strait as tensions escalate again.
US says the 'offensive phase' is over; Tehran will not be allowed to control Hormuz.
Commercial vessel seized off UAE and taken toward Iran; second ship attacked and sunk near Oman. Brent crude jumps 3.37% to $109.30.

theatres.name: Israel – Lebanon & Gaza
theatres.status: Ceasefire extended 45 days, but daily strikes continue

timeline.event: Conflict between Israel and Hezbollah intensifies in southern Lebanon; ground operations and air strikes displace hundreds of thousands.
US President Trump announces a Lebanon–Israel ceasefire to halt fighting with Hezbollah.
Gaza's deadliest month of 2026: 140+ reported fatalities; violent events up 35% vs March; Israel kills the head of Hamas military intelligence.
Israeli air strikes on Lebanon kill 41 people in 24 hours.
Israel issues new forced displacement orders in southern Lebanon, including beyond its current occupation zone.
US confirms productive Israel–Lebanon talks in Washington; second 45-day ceasefire extension agreed.
Israel strikes target Haddad, who Israeli officials say was undermining Trump's Gaza plan. Cumulative Lebanon toll: 2,800+ killed, 1M+ displaced (>20% of Lebanon's population).

theatres.name: Sudan Civil War
theatres.status: Year 4 — drone war escalating, pre-negotiation talks in Jeddah

timeline.event: Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) and the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces (RSF) begin civil war in Khartoum.
SAF retakes Khartoum from RSF; RSF retains control of Darfur and large parts of the west.
UN fact-finding mission identifies 'hallmarks of genocide' in Darfur.
Drones cause 80%+ of civilian deaths in Sudan in the first four months of 2026 — at least 880 killed.
Drone strikes on Al Quz, South Kordofan and near El Obeid kill 26 civilians.
30M Sudanese now need humanitarian aid; SAF and RSF delegates begin pre-negotiation talks in Jeddah, brokered by Saudi Arabia and the US.

hantavirus.label: Hantavirus Outbreak
hantavirus.ai_summary: The MV Hondius cruise-ship cluster has stabilised but is still serious: 11 cases, three deaths as of May 13, with the Andes hantavirus — the only hantavirus known to spread human-to-human — confirmed in two of the deaths. Passengers were repatriated from Tenerife to seven countries. WHO classifies the global risk as low and says it is 'not the start of another COVID'; the US CDC has it at a level 3 emergency response.

stories.headline: MV Hondius cluster: 11 cases, three deaths confirmed by WHO update
stories.summary: As of May 13, WHO and ECDC report 11 cases (8 confirmed, 1 inconclusive, 2 probable) tied to the MV Hondius, with three deaths — two confirmed as caused by Andes virus and one probable. The first case is believed to have been infected on land in South America before boarding the ship.

stories.headline: Ship reached Tenerife May 10; passengers repatriated to seven countries
stories.summary: After approval from Spanish health authorities, the MV Hondius sailed for Tenerife with 147 people aboard on May 6 and arrived May 10. Passengers were disembarked and flown home to six European countries and Canada under quarantine protocols, with international contact tracing ongoing.

stories.headline: WHO: outbreak is 'not the start of another COVID'
stories.summary: WHO has emphasised that the global public-health risk is low. Andes virus only spreads through close, sustained, human-to-human contact and has historically been contained to close-contact settings. However, the case fatality rate of hantavirus pulmonary syndrome remains high — 20–40% historically.

stories.headline: US CDC classifies the response as 'Level 3' emergency
stories.summary: The US CDC has classified the MV Hondius outbreak as a level 3 emergency response — its mid-tier activation level — and issued a HAN advisory to clinicians. CDC, WHO, ECDC and Spanish health authorities are coordinating epidemiological investigations with Argentina and Chile to trace the original land-based source.

cybersecurity.label: CyberSecurity
cybersecurity.ai_summary: It's a busy patch cycle: Cisco's SD-WAN controller is being hit by a CVSS-10 zero-day with a CISA deadline of May 17; Microsoft's Patch Tuesday plugged 120 bugs including a wormable Netlogon RCE; and CISA flagged a Linux kernel root-escalation bug (CVE-2026-31431) as actively exploited. The headline story for defenders is human, not technical: Google's threat-intel group caught the first confirmed AI-generated zero-day — a 2FA bypass aimed at mass exploitation — and stopped it before it shipped. Patch Cisco and Windows now; assume AI-generated exploits are the new normal.

stories.headline: Critical Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN auth-bypass exploited in zero-day attacks
stories.summary: Cisco warned of a critical authentication-bypass flaw in Catalyst SD-WAN Controllers that lets unauthenticated attackers gain admin privileges. Active exploitation has been observed. CISA added it to the KEV catalog with a federal patching deadline of May 17, 2026.

stories.headline: Microsoft May 2026 Patch Tuesday: 120 fixes including wormable Netlogon RCE
stories.summary: Microsoft's May Patch Tuesday addressed 120 flaws — 17 rated Critical, 14 of them RCE. The headliner is CVE-2026-41089, a stack-based buffer overflow in Windows Netlogon flagged as wormable; an unauthenticated attacker can run code against domain controllers. CVE-2026-41096 (DNS Client RCE) is also patched.

stories.headline: CISA adds Linux kernel root-escalation bug 'Fragnasia' to KEV
stories.summary: A local privilege-escalation flaw in the Linux kernel — dubbed Fragnasia — performs a 4-byte overwrite in the page cache to corrupt kernel-managed data and escalate to root. CISA added it to the KEV catalog with a federal remediation deadline of May 15, 2026. Major distros have shipped fixes.

stories.headline: Google catches first AI-generated zero-day: a 2FA bypass aimed at mass exploitation
stories.summary: Google's Threat Intelligence Group disrupted a planned mass-exploitation campaign that used an AI-generated Python exploit to bypass 2FA in a widely deployed open-source admin tool. Tell-tale signs included a hallucinated CVSS score, abundant 'textbook' docstrings, and clean LLM-style structure. Google says it is the first confirmed AI-built zero-day used in the wild.

stories.headline: Windows BitLocker zero-day with public PoC bypasses encrypted drives
stories.summary: A pair of Windows zero-days — codenamed YellowKey (BitLocker bypass) and GreenPlasma (CTFMON privilege escalation) — were disclosed with a working proof-of-concept. The BitLocker flaw lets a local attacker access protected drives. Both are awaiting full patches.

stories.headline: Instructure Canvas breach: ShinyHunters claim 275M student records stolen
stories.summary: Instructure, maker of the Canvas LMS, confirmed a cyber incident affecting its cloud environment. ShinyHunters claims it stole roughly 275 million records belonging to students, teachers, and staff across 8,809 institutions worldwide — described as the largest education-sector breach on record.

stories.headline: Foxconn hit by ransomware; attackers claim 11 million customer files stolen
stories.summary: Foxconn, the contract manufacturer behind devices for Apple, Google, Nvidia, Dell, and Intel, was hit by a cyberattack at its North America facilities. The attackers claim to have stolen more than 11 million files, including confidential information from major Foxconn customers.
